info.title: Convex Deployment API
info.description: Admin API for interacting with deployments.

post.description:
Update one or many environment variables in a deployment.
This will invalidate all subscriptions, since environment variables
are accessible in queries but are not part of the cache key of a query
result.

get.description:
Get all environment variables in a deployment.
In the future this might not include "secret" environment
variables.

List a deployment's audit log events on or after a timestamp, from least to
most recent. Pass the returned `cursor` (along with the same `from`) to
fetch the next page.

parameters.name: from
parameters.description:
Only return events on or after this time, in milliseconds since epoch.
Pass the same value on every page of a paginated request.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of events to return (defaults to 15, capped at 100).

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Cursor from a previous response to fetch the next page.

get.description: Returns identity information about this deployment.
Get all usage limit configs for a deployment.

post.description: Create a new usage limit config for a deployment.
Replace an existing usage limit config for a deployment.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: id of the usage limit to update

post.description: Delete an existing usage limit config for a deployment.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: id of the usage limit to delete

post.description:
Set or unset the canonical URL for a deployment's convex.cloud or
convex.site domain. This allows you to customize the  CONVEX_SITE_URL and
CONVEX_CLOUD_URL environment variables in your deployment.

get.description: Get the canonical URLs for a deployment.
List configs for all existing log streams in a deployment.
Get the config for a specific log stream by id.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: id of the log stream to retrieve

post.description: Delete the deployment's log stream with the given id.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: id of the log stream to delete

post.description:
Create a new log stream for the deployment. Errors if a log stream of the
given type already exists.

Update an existing log stream for the deployment. Omit a field to keep the
existing value, and use `null` to unset a field.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: id of the log stream to update

post.description: Rotate the secret for the webhook log stream.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: id of the webhook log stream for which to rotate the secret

post.description:
Disables a deployment without deleting any data. The deployment will not
operate until it is unpaused. While a deployment is paused, new functions
calls will return an error, scheduled jobs will queue and run when the
deployment is resumed, and cron jobs will be skipped. This means that no
function calls or bandwidth usage will be charged while the deployment is
paused, but storage costs will still apply.

Reenables a deployment that was previously paused. The deployment will
resume normal operation, including any scheduled jobs that were queued while
paused.

**Early access:** this API is not yet stable and may change in
backwards-incompatible ways without notice. Contact the Convex team before
depending on it.

Streams a consistent, resumable export of a deployment's data — either the
whole deployment or a subset of components, tables, and columns (see the
request body). Streaming export must be enabled on the deployment, and the
caller must have the `deployment:data:view` permission.

Call this endpoint repeatedly, passing the `cursor` from each response back
in the next request; omit `cursor` on the first call. The cursor is opaque —
store and send it back verbatim. Each response contains:

- `values`: document revisions in the order they should be applied. A value
  with `_deleted: true` is a tombstone marking that document as deleted.
- `truncates`: tables whose contents were replaced wholesale (for example by
  an `npx convex import`). Drop everything you have stored for each listed
  table; the `values` in this and later responses re-populate it.
- `status`: `inProgress` while the export is still being assembled — the
  data returned so far is not yet a consistent view, so keep calling. Once
  it becomes `synced`, the values applied so far form a consistent snapshot
  of the deployment as of the returned `snapshot` timestamp. You can keep
  calling to continue streaming later changes; `hasMore` tells you whether
  more data is already available (`true`) or you've caught up to the latest
  commit (`false`).

Persist the cursor and keep calling within the deployment's data retention
window so the export can resume where it left off. This endpoint must be
called at least once every 3 days; if too much time passes between calls the
cursor falls outside the retention window and can no longer be resumed. When
that happens the endpoint responds with a `400` (`DataSyncCursorExpired`),
and you must restart the sync from scratch by calling again with no cursor.

oneOf.title: Member
oneOf.description: A member acting via the dashboard.

oneOf.title: Token
oneOf.description: A deploy key or access token.

client_id.description: Client ID of the OAuth application, if the token belongs to one.

oneOf.description: Member ID the token belongs to, if any.

token_id.description:
Token ID. `0` for legacy audit log rows created before token IDs
were recorded.

AuditLogActor.description: The identity that performed an audit log action.

attributes.description:
Optional list of attributes. These are extra fields and values sent to
Axiom in each log event.

datasetName.description: Name of the dataset in Axiom. This is where the logs will be sent.

topics.description:
The topics this log stream is subscribed to. `null` means subscribed to
all topics, including ones added in the future.

attributes.description:
Optional list of attributes. These are extra fields and values sent to
Axiom in each log event.

datasetName.description: Name of the dataset in Axiom. This is where the logs will be sent.

topics.description:
The topics this log stream is subscribed to. Omit to
subscribe to all topics, including ones added in the future.

ddTags.description:
Optional comma-separated list of tags. These are sent to Datadog in each
log event via the `ddtags` field.

service.description: Service name used as a special tag in Datadog.

topics.description:
The topics this log stream is subscribed to. Omit to
subscribe to all topics, including ones added in the future.

host.description: PostHog host URL. Defaults to https://us.i.posthog.com.
PostHog host URL. Defaults to https://us.i.posthog.com.

serviceName.description: OTLP service.name attribute. Defaults to the deployment name.

topics.description:
The topics this log stream is subscribed to. Omit to
subscribe to all topics, including ones added in the future.

dsn.description: Sentry Data Source Name (DSN) to route exceptions to.

tags.description: Tags to add to all events routed to Sentry.

format.description:
Format for the webhook payload. JSONL sends one object per line of
request, JSON sends one array per request.

topics.description:
The topics this log stream is subscribed to. Omit to
subscribe to all topics, including ones added in the future.

hmacSecret.description: Use this secret to verify webhook signatures.

allOf.description:
The components, tables, and columns to export. When omitted, everything
is exported. Supports the shorthand forms `{"tableName": "...",
"component": "..."}` and `{"component": "..."}`, or the exact form
`{"selection": {...}}` (a map of component -> table -> column
inclusion).

cursor.description: Opaque cursor returned by a previous call. Omit to start from scratch.

DataSyncArgs.description: Arguments to the data sync (streaming export) API (`/api/v1/data/sync`).

DataSyncProgress.description: Progress indicator returned while a data sync is `InProgress`.

DataSyncResponse.description: One page returned by the data sync API.

cursor.description: Opaque cursor to pass back in as `cursor` on the next call.

status.description: The consistency state of the sync after this page.

truncates.description:
Tables truncated by this page: the consumer should drop everything it
previously synced for each, then apply `values` (which re-sync them from
scratch). Logically applies before `values`.

values.description: Documents and tombstones produced by this page.

oneOf.description:
The entries emitted so far represent a consistent snapshot at
`snapshot`. The cursor can be persisted and used to continue the sync
later (within the document retention window).

hasMore.description:
Whether `snapshot` is behind the latest timestamp — i.e. it's a
consistent snapshot but not fully caught up to the most recent
commit. Callers use this to decide whether to keep calling the API
or pause until later.

allOf.description: More pages are required before the view is consistent.

oneOf.description: More pages are required before the view is consistent.

DataSyncStatus.description: The consistency state reported alongside a data sync page.

DataSyncTruncate.description:
A table whose contents were replaced wholesale (e.g. by `npx convex
import`). Reported separately from `values` since it carries none of the
per-document fields.

_component.description: The path of the component the table is in.

_table.description: The name of the truncated table.

allOf.description:
The fields of the document. Connectors must ignore fields prefixed by
`_` (except `_id` and `_creationTime`) since they could be used by
future versions of the API for new fields. For tombstones, only `_id`
is present.

_component.description: The path of the component this entry is from.

_deleted.description: Whether the document was deleted (a tombstone).

_table.description: The name of the table this entry is from.

_ts.description: The timestamp at which this revision was written.

DataSyncValue.description:
A single document-level entry emitted by the data sync API: a Convex
document (or a tombstone, for a deletion) with some special fields added.

ddTags.description:
Optional comma-separated list of tags. These are sent to Datadog in each
log event via the `ddtags` field.

service.description: Service name used as a special tag in Datadog.

topics.description:
The topics this log stream is subscribed to. `null` means subscribed to
all topics, including ones added in the future.

DatadogSiteLocation.description: The Datadog deployment locations, used to construct URLs

actor.description: The identity that performed the action.

clientIp.description: IP address of the client that performed the action, if known.

clientUserAgent.description: User agent of the client that performed the action, if known.

createTime.description: Time the event was created, in milliseconds since epoch.

metadata.description: Additional JSON metadata about the audit log event.

items.description: The audit log events for this page, from least to most recent.

topics.description:
The topics this log stream is subscribed to. `null` means subscribed to
all topics, including ones added in the future.

tags.description: Tags to add to all events routed to Sentry.

attributes.description:
Optional list of attributes. These are extra fields and values sent to
Axiom in each log event.

datasetName.description: Name of the dataset in Axiom. This is where the logs will be sent.

topics.description:
The topics this log stream is subscribed to. Omit to keep the current
subscription, or pass `null` to subscribe to all topics (including ones
added in the future).

requestDestination.description: Whether to update the canonical URL for convex.cloud or convex.site

url.description:
The new canonical URL. Omit this to reset the canonical URl to the
default value.

ddTags.description:
Optional comma-separated list of tags. These are sent to Datadog in each
log event via the `ddtags` field.

service.description: Service name used as a special tag in Datadog.

topics.description:
The topics this log stream is subscribed to. Omit to keep the current
subscription, or pass `null` to subscribe to all topics (including ones
added in the future).

Format for the webhook payload. JSONL sends one object per line of

topics.description:

Deploy Key.name: Authorization
Deploy Key.description: Deploy keys are used for deployment operations. See [deploy key types](https://docs.convex.dev/cli/deploy-key-types) for more information. Use the `Convex ` prefix (e.g., `Convex <deploy_key>`).

OAuth Project Token.name: Authorization
OAuth Project Token.description: Obtained through a [Convex OAuth application](https://docs.convex.dev/management-api) with project scope. Use the `Convex ` prefix (e.g., `Convex <oauth_project_token>`).

OAuth Team Token.name: Authorization
OAuth Team Token.description: Obtained through a [Convex OAuth application](https://docs.convex.dev/management-api). Use the `Convex ` prefix (e.g., `Convex <oauth_token>`).

Team Token.name: Authorization
Team Token.description: Created in the dashboard under team settings for any team you can manage. Use the `Convex ` prefix (e.g., `Convex <team_token>`).
